question: Which countries have the highest percentage contribution from agriculture to their GDP, and how does this compare to their overall GDP values?

analysis.design_rationale: A scatter plot with agriculture sector % as the y-axis and total GDP as the x-axis provides a holistic view of the relationship between economic scale and agricultural reliance. Color encoding via a continuous colormap visually identifies countries with very high agriculture % and allows for discovery of patterns such as whether lower GDP countries are more reliant on agriculture. This visualization makes excellent use of numeric fields for both axes and helps answer the comparative aspect of the question.
analysis.target_insight: This visualization reveals whether countries with high agriculture sector % also have high or low overall GDP, showing correlations and outliers.

chart_encodings.other_aesthetics: Country names for top outliers labeled; Color mapped to agriculture %

transformations: Convert df['Agriculture Sector GDP of % Contribution'] to float
Drop rows with missing values in either field
Identify top outlier countries with highest agriculture sector % for labeling purposes
data_preparation_steps: df['Agriculture Sector GDP of % Contribution'] = pd.to_numeric(df['Agriculture Sector GDP of % Contribution'], errors='coerce')
df_scatter = df.dropna(subset=['Agriculture Sector GDP of % Contribution', 'GDP (millions of $)'])
df_outliers = df_scatter.sort_values(by='Agriculture Sector GDP of % Contribution', ascending=False).head(4)
suggestion.matplotlib_code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib as mpl
df = pd.read_csv("csv_c_squared\1.csv")
df['Agriculture Sector GDP of % Contribution'] = pd.to_numeric(df['Agriculture Sector GDP of % Contribution'], errors='coerce')
df['GDP (millions of $)'] = pd.to_numeric(df['GDP (millions of $)'], errors='coerce')
df_scatter = df.dropna(subset=['Agriculture Sector GDP of % Contribution', 'GDP (millions of $)'])
cmap = mpl.colormaps['viridis']
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10,7))
sc = ax.scatter(df_scatter['GDP (millions of $)'], df_scatter['Agriculture Sector GDP of % Contribution'], c=df_scatter['Agriculture Sector GDP of % Contribution'], cmap=cmap, edgecolor="black", s=70, alpha=0.7)
cb = plt.colorbar(sc, ax=ax)
cb.set_label("Agriculture Sector % of GDP")
ax.set_xscale("log")
ax.set_xlabel("Total GDP (millions of $) [Log Scale]")
ax.set_ylabel("Agriculture Sector % of GDP")
plt.title("Relationship between Agriculture Sector % of GDP and Total GDP\n(Colored by Agriculture Sector %)", fontsize=16)
df_outliers = df_scatter.sort_values(by='Agriculture Sector GDP of % Contribution', ascending=False).head(4)
for _, row in df_outliers.iterrows():
    ax.text(row['GDP (millions of $)'], row['Agriculture Sector GDP of % Contribution'] + 0.7, row['Country/Economy'], fontsize=10, fontweight='bold', ha='center', va='bottom', color='firebrick')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
